response:
First, recall the formula for momentum: momentum \( p = m \times v \), where \( m \) is mass and \( v \) is velocity.
1.  Identify the given values:
    - Mass of the baseball \( m = 0.15\ \text{kg} \)
    - Velocity of the baseball \( v = 40\ \text{m/s} \)
2.  Calculate momentum:
    \( p = 0.15\ \text{kg} \times 40\ \text{m/s} = 6.0\ \text{kg} \times \text{m/s} \)

This matches option C.
Answer: C
